title: 10 best herbal tinctures for upset stomach

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The tannins present in yellow gentian work to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining, alleviating symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea. Additionally, yellow gentian's bitter compounds stimulate digestion, improving the movement of food through the digestive system and reducing discomfort. As a result, herbal yellow gentian tinctures offer effective relief from upset stomachs and indigestion.
remedy_properties: Bitter principles: Yellow Gentian's bitter compounds stimulate digestion, improve appetite, and relieve digestive discomfort by increasing saliva production and gut motility.
Antispasmodic properties: The tincture's antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing cramps, spasms, and pain associated with upset stomach.
Anti-inflammatory activity: Yellow Gentian's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe and protect the digestive lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to stomach upset.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds, particularly gentiopicroside and swertiamarin, have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and relax the smooth muscles in the stomach, alleviating nausea and abdominal cramps.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as gentisic acid and ferulic acid, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to protect the stomach lining from damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of nausea and stomach upset.

Alkaloids: Specifically, gentianine and gentisin, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation, kill off bacteria that may be contributing to stomach upset, and alleviate symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of bioactive compounds, including gentiopicroside, which helps to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues.
Leaves: They contain various bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can aid in digestive health.

Roots: Again, roots are mentioned as they are the most commonly used part for making tinctures due to their high gentiopicroside content, which is believed to have a positive effect on digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried gentiana lutea root in the late summer or early fall season.
Chop the dried root into small pieces to increase its surface area for infusion.
Combine 1 part of chopped gentiana lutea root with 2 parts of vodka in a glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking it every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with upset stomach because it contains compounds that soothe and calm the digestive system. The flavonoids and glycyrrhizin present in licorice root have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach lining. This, in turn, relieves symptoms such as heartburn, acid reflux, and nausea. Additionally, licorice tinctures can stimulate digestion and absorption of nutrients, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and alleviating digestive discomfort. By taking herbal licorice tinctures, individuals may experience rapid relief from stomach upset and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: A compound found in licorice root that has anti-inflammatory and antacid properties, helping to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Flavonoids: Licorice tincture contains flavonoids such as liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to calm digestive irritation and reduce oxidative stress in the gut.

Saponins: The saponin content in licorice root has been shown to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, reducing acid production and inflammation, while also promoting the healing of ulcers and other gastrointestinal lesions.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with upset stomach by inhibiting the production of stomach acid and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of heartburn and indigestion.

Licopyranocoumarin: This phenolic compound helps soothe and calm the digestive system by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which reduces irritation and discomfort in the stomach and intestines.

18α-Glycyrrhetinic acid: It assists in calming the stomach and reducing inflammation by blocking the action of the hormone aldosterone, which helps in regulating fluid and electrolyte balance in the body, thereby alleviating symptoms of stomach upset.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to soothe the stomach.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain flavonoids and saponins, which can help to calm digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra by hand in the morning or evening.
Clean the root material by gently brushing off dirt and debris with a small brush for 5 minutes.
Chop the cleaned root into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter for 10 minutes.
Steep the chopped root material in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar for 4 weeks.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with nausea and indigestion. Additionally, ginger's warming properties help stimulate digestion and relieve cramps, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_properties: Digestive Enzyme Stimulant: Ginger tincture stimulates digestive enzymes, such as gastric amylase, lipase, and trypsin, which helps to break down proteins, carbohydrates, and fats, alleviating symptoms of indigestion and nausea.

Anti-Inflammatory Compound: Ginger contains a potent anti-inflammatory compound called gingerol, which reduces inflammation in the digestive tract and relieves pain and discomfort associated with stomach upset, such as cramps and bloating.

Musculotropic Agent: Ginger's musculotropic properties help to relax and reduce spasms in the smooth muscle of the digestive tract, which can calm symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), diarrhea, and stomach cramps.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: A type of phenolic compound, gingerol helps with upset stomach by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause inflammation and pain in the digestive tract.

Shogaol: A bioactive compound derived from ginger, shogaol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe the digestive system and reduce nausea and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.

Zingiberene: A sesquiterpene, zingiberene has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to relax the digestive muscles and reduce inflammation in the stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms of upset stomach such as cramps and bloating.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They contain the highest concentration of active compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and digestive properties.
Roots: The roots are also a rich source of active compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which help to soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea.
Buds: The buds of the Zingiber officinale plant contain a high concentration of essential oils, including gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient amount of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with approximately 100 grams for the recipe.
Clean and slice the rhizomes into thin pieces using a sharp knife with a clean surface.
Combine the sliced rhizomes with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar with a 1:5 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar occasionally every week.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel from the aloe plant contains mucilages, which are natural emollients that can help calm digestive irritation and reduce inflammation in the gut. When taken as a tincture, these compounds are quickly absorbed into the bloodstream and travel to the stomach where they can provide relief from symptoms such as nausea, bloating, and cramps. This can help restore balance to the digestive system and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), constipation, and other gastrointestinal issues.

Soil-antibacterial properties: The sulfated glycoproteins in aloe vera have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as E. coli, Salmonella, and Shigella, which can cause gut infections and stomach upset, while also promoting a healthy gut microbiome.

Gastroprotective effects: Aloe vera's mucilages and polysaccharides help soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract, reducing irritation and damage caused by stomach acid and digestive enzymes, which can alleviate symptoms of heartburn, bloating, and stomach discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: Anthraquinones, such as aloe-emodin, help with upset stomach by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting the movement of food through the digestive system.
Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, particularly acemannan, help with upset stomach by stimulating the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, reducing inflammation, and promoting healing of the gut lining.
Saponins: Saponins, such as aloin, help with upset stomach by reducing inflammation, protecting the gut lining from damage, and preventing the growth of pathogenic bacteria in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe vera contain high amounts of gel, which is used to create soothing tinctures to calm stomach issues.
Barks: The barks of Aloe vera contain compounds that help in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the stomach lining.
Roots: The roots of Aloe vera are known to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help in reducing stomach pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 to 20 aloe vera leaves with thick green skin and store them in a cool dark place for 2 days.
Cut the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and dry them in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine the dried aloe vera pieces with 60 percent 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar in a 1:5 ratio.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and shake it every day for 2 weeks to allow the extraction process to complete.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of trigonella foenum graecum flowers at the peak of blooming during the warmest part of the day.
Clean the flowers thoroughly with a soft brush to remove any dirt or debris that may be present.
Combine the clean flowers with 750ml of 70% ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal the container tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks, shaking the jar every 2 days to ensure even extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids and label the tincture.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.

Combine the dried flowers with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.

remedy_properties: Silymarin: Helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause upset stomach symptoms.

Siliphen: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a powerful flavonoid compound that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of isoschaftole, a phytosterol that has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried milk thistle seeds in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Combine one part of the dried milk thistle seeds with five parts of organic vodka or grain alcohol.
Transfer the seed and solvent mixture into a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and label it properly.
Allow the mixture to macerate for at least four weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm digestive issues. The plant's mucilaginous properties create a protective barrier in the gut, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause discomfort, bloating, and cramps. Additionally, plantain's gentle astringent action helps to restore the balance of gut flora, promoting healthy digestion and absorption of nutrients. As a result, plantain tinctures provide quick relief from stomach upset, calming digestive issues and promoting overall gut health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
Sothing and calming effects: Plantain contains mucilage, a type of fiber that forms a protective barrier on the stomach lining, soothing and calming irritations and reducing discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Plantain's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals in the digestive tract, which can contribute to oxidative stress and damage, leading to upset stomach symptoms such as bloating and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in treating digestive issues such as upset stomach.
Barks: Plantain barks contain compounds that help to calm inflammation in the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried plantago major leaves in abundance for processing.
Cleanse the harvested plantago major leaves of dirt debris and other impurities thoroughly.
Chop the cleaned plantago major leaves into small pieces ensuring uniformity throughout.
Soak the chopped plantago major leaves in 80% ethanol at a ratio of 1:5 for 14 days.
Strain the resulting liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curly dock tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its soothing properties, which calm irritated digestive tracts. The herbal extract contains anthraquinones, a type of compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and ease spasms in the intestinal tract. This helps to alleviate symptoms such as cramping, bloating, and diarrhea. Additionally, curly dock's natural anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce irritation and discomfort associated with stomach upset, promoting overall digestive health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Curly dock tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach such as bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Antioxidant activity: The tannins and phenolic acids present in curly dock tincture exhibit antioxidant properties, which help neutralize free radicals and protect the mucous membranes lining the digestive tract from oxidative stress, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.

Soothing and protective mucilages: Curly dock contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that coats and soothes the digestive tract, providing relief from inflammation, irritation, and discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of curly dock contain anthraquinones, which help soothe and calm the digestive system, making them an effective remedy for upset stomach.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of curly dock have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rumex crispus leaves and stems on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Clean the harvested material by gently washing it with filtered water and pat drying with paper towels.
Chop the cleaned material into small pieces and pack it tightly into a clean glass jar leaving about one inch headspace.
Pour high proof vodka or grain alcohol over the plant material to fill the jar and ensure complete coverage.
Allow the tincture to steep for at least six weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar once a week for optimal extraction.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for upset stomach are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate digestive discomforts, reducing inflammation and calming the digestive system. The best herbal tinctures for upset stomach include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Mentha x piperita, Aloe barbadensis, Cinchona officinalis, Peumus boldus, Angelica archangelica, Aloe vera, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, upset stomach can lead to weight loss, fatigue, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being, making these remedies essential for maintaining digestive health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to upset stomach that are treated with herbal tinctures are digestive issues such as irritable bowel syndrome, nausea and vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, and bloating. Herbal remedies like peppermint, ginger, chamomile, and licorice root tinctures can help soothe the digestive system, calm inflammation, and ease symptoms associated with these conditions. These natural treatments are often gentle on the stomach and may be more effective than over-the-counter medications in some cases.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with upset stomach is a blend of Peppermint, Ginger, and Chamomile. Peppermint tincture soothes digestive issues and calms nausea, while Ginger helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramps. Chamomile, with its anti-inflammatory properties, promotes relaxation and eases digestion. This combination can be taken in small doses, 2-3 times a day, mixed with water or a carrier oil, and may help to alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach and promote overall digestive health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe digestive discomfort. The active compound, gingerol, helps to relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing nausea and spasms. Additionally, ginger tincture aids in digestion and absorption of nutrients, reducing bloating and cramping. Its warming and stimulating effects also help to calm the nervous system, further alleviating symptoms of an upset stomach and promoting overall digestive well-being. This makes ginger tincture a popular natural remedy for stomach issues.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Gingerol helps with upset stomach by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting.
Shogaol: Shogaol helps with upset stomach by inhibiting the production of stomach acid and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate heartburn and indigestion.
Zingerone: Zingerone helps with upset stomach by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the digestive tract, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Buds: Ginger buds, which are the young, unopened shoots of the plant, are used to make tinctures due to their high content of bioactive compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which help alleviate stomach upset.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale roots in late summer or early fall when they are mature and dry.
Chop the harvested zingiber officinale roots into small pieces to release their essential oils and resin.
Combine the chopped roots with 80% vodka 20% water mixture in a 1:5 ratio by weight to create the solvent.
Allow the mixture to steep in a glass container for 2-6 weeks shaking it occasionally to ensure even extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with upset stomach because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture soothes the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation that can cause stomach discomfort. Its expectorant properties also help to clear out mucus and excess fluids, allowing the stomach to return to its natural digestive processes. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to stimulate the production of stomach acid, which is essential for proper digestion and relief from upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help to soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
Glycyrrhizin: It helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach, and also has a mild anti-inflammatory effect that can help alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
Flavonoids: They exhibit antioxidant properties that can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the stomach, contributing to the relief of upset stomach symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.

Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures as they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and carminative effects, helping to alleviate digestive issues.
Stems: The stems of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures as they contain glycosides and saponins that have soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to calm the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried root of the herb in late summer or early fall.
Chop the dried root into small pieces and dry them further for 2 hours.
Combine the dried root with 80% ethanol in a ratio of 1:5 and let it steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its carminative and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Foeniculum vulgare, such as anethole and fenchone, have been shown to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. This leads to a decrease in symptoms of upset stomach, including bloating, cramping, and nausea. Additionally, the tincture's ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract helps to alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of calm, making it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Fenchone: It has anti-spasmodic properties, helping to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate cramps associated with upset stomach.
Anethole: It has carminative properties, helping to reduce gas and bloating, which are common causes of discomfort and pain in the stomach.
Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid): They possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe digestive issues.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to ease digestive discomfort, reduce inflammation, and promote the healing of the digestive tract.
Stems: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to alleviate digestive problems and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried foeniculum vulgare by cutting the stems at the base of the plant when in full flower.
Clean and dry the foeniculum vulgare thoroughly to prevent contamination and ensure quality of the tincture.
Combine 1 part of foeniculum vulgare with 2 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe irritated stomach linings. The tincture's essential oils, particularly menthol and menthone, can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms. Additionally, its carminative properties can ease gas and bloating, allowing the body to digest food more efficiently. As a result, mentha x piperita tinctures can provide quick relief from stomach discomfort and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Acts as a local anesthetic, numbing the stomach lining and reducing inflammation, which can help alleviate stomach pain and discomfort.
Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, contributing to the relief of upset stomach symptoms.

Methanol and ethanol derived compounds (e.g. menthone, limonene): Have a sedative effect on the stomach, reducing spasms and promoting digestion, which can help alleviate nausea, vomiting, and other symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have natural anti-inflammatory and calming properties.
Roots: Utilized for their robust flavor and aroma, as well as their ability to stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea.
Stems: Employed for their rich content of menthone and other compounds that help soothe the stomach and alleviate cramps.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers for tincture preparation.
Combine the fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers with 750 milliliters of 45% ethanol in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids.
Label and store the mentha x piperita tincture in a dark glass bottle away from children and pets.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with upset stomach because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The tannins and anthraquinones present in Aloe vera help to reduce inflammation and irritation, promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria. Additionally, Aloe barbadensis tinctures have been shown to increase the production of mucus in the stomach lining, protecting it from acid and reducing symptoms of heartburn and indigestion. This makes it a natural remedy for soothing an upset stomach.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to an upset stomach.
Aloin: This anthraquinone glycoside helps to stimulate the movement of food through the digestive system, reducing cramps and bloating associated with an upset stomach.

Fatty acids: The fatty acids present in Aloe barbadensis tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Aloe barbadensis contain gel and latex, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm an upset stomach.
Roots: The roots of Aloe barbadensis contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Buds: Aloe barbadensis buds contain saponins and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an irritated stomach and promote digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe barbadensis leaves with a sharp knife when they are young and tender for best potency.
Chop the aloe leaves into small pieces and weigh out 1 part of the leaves to 2 parts of vodka.
Combine the chopped aloe leaves and vodka in a clean glass jar and seal the lid tightly.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every week.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties. The tannins and other compounds present in the tincture have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and calm the stomach. The tincture's ability to reduce acid production and alleviate symptoms associated with digestive discomfort makes it a popular remedy for upset stomachs. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis tincture may help to regulate bowel movements and ease nausea, providing relief from stomach upset.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: It helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramping in the stomach, providing relief from nausea and diarrhea associated with upset stomach.
Quinidine: This constituent has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe the stomach and reduce symptoms of upset stomach such as bloating and cramps.

Alkaloids (Cinchonine and Cinchonidine): They help to reduce inflammation and slow down the contractions of the stomach, thereby providing relief from nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea associated with upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Alkaloids present in the leaves, particularly quinine, help in alleviating digestive issues and reducing inflammation.
Barks: The barks of Cinchona officinalis contain quinine and other alkaloids that aid in treating stomach upsets, nausea, and diarrhea.
Stems: The stems of the plant also contain quinine and other alkaloids, which are used to create tinctures for soothing stomach issues and relieving nausea.
remedy_recipe: Harvest cinchona officinalis leaves and stems when they are fresh and at the peak of potency for best results.
Dry the harvested cinchona officinalis plant material in a warm dry place for 2 to 3 weeks.
Combine 1 part dried cinchona officinalis plant material with 2 parts 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow extraction to occur.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate solids from liquid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peumus boldus tinctures helps with upset stomach because it contains a compound called boldine, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and cramping in the stomach and intestines. The tincture also has a gentle, relaxing effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for smoother digestion and elimination. This can provide quick relief from nausea, bloating, and other symptoms associated with an upset stomach.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds help alleviate stomach upset by reducing inflammation and spasms in the digestive tract.
Terpenes: Terpenes in Peumus boldus tinctures help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of nausea and abdominal pain.
Phenolic glycosides: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect the stomach lining from damage and reduce symptoms of stomach upset.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Peumus boldus are commonly used to make tinctures for upset stomach due to their high concentration of active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.

Roots: The roots of Peumus boldus are also used to make tinctures for upset stomach, as they contain a higher concentration of boldine, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and carminative effects.
Barks: The bark of Peumus boldus is sometimes used to make tinctures for upset stomach, as it contains a mixture of compounds that may help to soothe and calm the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh leaves of peumus boldus in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure maximum potency.
Dry the freshly harvested leaves in a single layer at a temperature below 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Combine 250 grams of the dried leaves with 750 milliliters of 45 percent ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for two weeks in a cool dark place with occasional gentle shaking for 5 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica tinctures helps with upset stomach because of its natural antispasmodic properties. It soothes digestive issues by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the stomach and intestines. The tincture also contains antioxidants that help protect the stomach lining from damage, promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria. As a result, Angelica archangelica tinctures can provide relief from nausea, bloating, and other symptoms associated with an upset stomach, promoting a sense of calm and well-being. This makes it a popular natural remedy for digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic glycosides: These compounds help to soothe and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.
Terpenoids (specifically, sesquiterpenes): These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to calm the stomach and prevent further irritation.

Acridone alkaloids (specifically, acorigenin and acorin): These alkaloids have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which help to ease stomach cramps and discomfort associated with upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as angelic root, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach.
Leaves: Containing volatile oils like angelic acid, which has carminative properties that ease digestive discomfort and reduce gas.
Stems: Enriched with sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory effects that may help alleviate stomach pain and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves in the early morning after dew has evaporated and before the heat of the day.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently rinsing with cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the cleaned plant material in a low-temperature dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours to preserve its potency.
Combine one part dried plant material with five parts 80 proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar to create a 1:5 ratio.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day to extract the medicinal properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures helps with upset stomach because it contains a soothing compound called aloin, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to calm irritated tissues in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Aloe vera also has a gentle, non-irritating effect on the stomach lining, allowing it to heal and repair itself. Additionally, it aids in digestion and promotes the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, further alleviating stomach upset and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties and help to reduce inflammation in the stomach, alleviating symptoms of upset stomach.
Phenolic acids: They have antioxidant properties that help to protect the stomach lining from damage and promote healing, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects that help to calm the stomach and reduce irritation, alleviating symptoms of an upset stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from Aloe vera leaves is used to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms of an upset stomach.
Flowers: The flowers of Aloe vera are used to create a tincture that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate stomach pain and discomfort.
Stems: The juice from the stems of Aloe vera is used to create a tincture that has a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera leaves in the early morning when the plants are at their highest water content.
Clean and dry the aloe vera leaves thoroughly to remove any dirt or impurities from the surface.
Chop the dried aloe vera leaves into small pieces and weigh out 500 grams for the tincture.
Combine the chopped aloe vera pieces with 750 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the aloe vera and ethanol mixture to macerate for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with upset stomach because it has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The flavonoids and terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba tinctures have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the stomach, promoting better digestion and reducing discomfort. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has been traditionally used to alleviate nausea and improve appetite, making it a natural remedy for upset stomachs. Its anti-inflammatory effects also help to reduce stomach cramps and spasms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These help to alleviate stomach discomfort by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the digestive system.
Bilobalide: This constituent helps to soothe the stomach by reducing spasms and alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
Quercetin: This flavonoid helps to protect the stomach lining from damage and inflammation, reducing the risk of ulcers and other stomach problems.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part in tinctures due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which can help soothe and calm the digestive system.
Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds may be used in tinctures as they contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate stomach discomfort.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds can be used in tinctures for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate stomach upset and promote digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable source and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush.
Combine 1 part dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the ginkgo biloba tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years, shaking the container before use.
